title: lycianthes subfalcata
intro: Lycianthes subfalcata, also known as the shrubby or wild potato, is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family, Solanaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lycianthes subfalcata include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Lycianthes subfalcata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
